body:
**Scope:** promote the test-invoked ledger sweep (`core::sweep_expired_leases`) into a **running GC custodian** with two inputs (§6.7): expired **pending-ledger leases** (crashed writes/repairs — the leased garbage M1/M2 produce on failed fan-out) and **orphaned fragments** (from deletes and completed reconstructions). Reclaim bytes via the new `delete_fragment` **only after a reader-safe grace window** — long enough that an in-flight reader holding the prior version is never torn (the pending-ledger sweep pattern, §5). Emit GC telemetry + audit events. The invariant whose violation is silent corruption: **never reclaim a referenced fragment.**

**Definition of done**
- [ ] leased garbage (expired pending leases) and orphaned fragment bytes are reclaimed by the running loop
- [ ] a fragment referenced by a committed chunk map is **never** deleted
- [ ] an in-flight reader holding the prior version is **never** torn (grace window honoured)
- [ ] GC actions appear in the append-only audit/event log

**Needs:** M3.2, M3.3. **Refs:** proposal 0005 §"The four custodian loops" (GC); architecture §6.7, §5.

milestone.title: M3 — Custodians
milestone.description: The self-maintaining durability plane (L4): GC, scrub, reconstruction, rebalance + durability telemetry. Makes single-zone trustworthy; gates the Step-2 release. Plan: proposal 0005.

title: M3.4 — GC custodian: expired leases + orphaned fragments, reader-safe grace window
